title: Add jitter to TTLs
description: Randomize per-entry TTLs so bulk-populated keys do not all expire on the same tick.
body: Use when many entries are populated together (cold start, bulk warm-up). Do not assign every entry an identical TTL — synchronized expiry creates a coordinated stampede when they all lapse at once. Actions: add randomized jitter (for example base ± 10-20%) to each TTL so expirations spread across a window, and record the chosen base and spread. Validation signals include expiry timestamps distributed across the window rather than clustered on one boundary. Risk boundary: too-short TTLs raise origin load while too-long ones extend staleness — verify the base against the data's tolerated freshness before rollout.
